moonshine-js — vendored
========================

Upstream:   https://github.com/moonshine-ai/moonshine
Package:    @moonshine-ai/moonshine-js
License:    MIT

Status
------
VENDORED — @moonshine-ai/moonshine-js@0.1.29 (see VERSION). Pulled +
SHA-verified by scripts/vendor-moonshine.sh (EXPECTED_DIST_SHA256 is
pinned there). Re-run that script to bump; do not pull "@latest".

What's here
-----------
  - moonshine.js           (the npm dist/moonshine.min.js verbatim, PLUS a
                            one-line `export const VENDORED = true;` sentinel
                            appended at the end — peerd's transcriber.js imports
                            { MicrophoneTranscriber, VENDORED }, and the upstream
                            dist doesn't provide VENDORED. The appended line is
                            the only modification to the upstream bytes.)
  - moonshine.js.SHA256    (the UPSTREAM dist hash, pre-append — tamper floor)
  - VERSION                (the pinned npm release the bytes came from)

Why vendored, not lazy-imported from CDN
----------------------------------------
MV3 CSP forbids remote script execution
(script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'). The moonshine JS itself must
live in the extension package. So must its ORT runtime + the Silero
VAD: Moonshine ships those pointing at jsdelivr
(Settings.BASE_ASSET_PATH.{ONNX_RUNTIME,SILERO_VAD}), and ORT's
cross-origin loader re-wraps the remote script as a blob:, which the
CSP blocks — voice then silently dies ("not supported"). peerd vendors
ORT 1.22 + vad-web 0.0.24 locally (../onnxruntime-web, ../vad-web) and
redirects those two Settings paths to same-origin in
peerd-runtime/voice/transcriber.js (init). ONLY the model .onnx bytes
(which are DATA, not script) are fetched at runtime from the configured
CDN — see peerd-runtime/voice/model-store.js for that path.

The model bytes are ~250MB for the shipped `base` variant and don't
need to ship with the extension — they're cached in IndexedDB on
first activation and reused thereafter.

Update procedure
----------------
1. Edit scripts/vendor-moonshine.sh to bump the pinned commit / tag.
2. Run the script. It downloads, verifies the upstream hash, writes
   moonshine.js + SHA256 + VERSION.
3. Commit the new bytes. The .SHA256 file is the tamper-detection
   floor — anything that disagrees with it on disk is a red flag.
4. Test locally (load unpacked, enable voice, transcribe a test phrase)
   before tagging a release.

For model .onnx file integrity (separate concern), see
peerd-runtime/voice/model-store.js. SRI hashes there pin specific
Hugging Face commits of UsefulSensors/moonshine.
